texto: If humans are indeed products of a design, is it not wise to make humans selfish? A selfish person safeguards what’s important to him and never exchanges it to one with a lesser value. If the salvation of his soul is the most important to him, he will do everything to secure it. An unselfish person who values his soul just as much is ready to exchange his salvation for less. They are self-deniers, right?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de H.R.Valderrama que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “If humans are indeed products of a design, is it not wise to make humans selfish? A selfish perso…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, altruism, altruist, com foco em mudanca e altruist.
